block.moretraffic.control_cabinet.descri: Can be used to make automations and to program lights in one block
block.moretraffic.blinker.description: When powered with redstone, it lights up. Paint to change colors
item.moretraffic.light.description: When used on a traffic pole, makes advanced traffic light. Click again to add more lights to traffic light. Sneak-click with wrench to remove one light
Scale of traffic light (TO BE IMPLEMENTED IN FUTURE)
gui.moretraffic.import_invalid_data: No valid data in clipboard, please make sure you copied the exported NBT correctly
gui.moretraffic.advanced_traffic_light.o:
Choose a static color for this light, or click black to turn it off.
Keep in mind that using control cabinet may override this color.
Using masking, shapes or pictograms (turning arrows, pedestrian icon, etc.) can be displayed on lights. Unlike in trafficcraft, masks can be unique for each light.
Start by drawing your mask, or use the buttons on the right to import or export

Left mouse button: click and hold to add pixels,
Right mouse button: click and hold to delete pixels
Load a mask from your clipboard
Copy the mask data to your clipboard. This is a string of numbers, so you can paste it outside of the game too
Turn an 16x16 image into a mask
For best results, use a 16x16 image with transparent and filled pixels. May not work perfectly.
gui.moretraffic.advanced_traffic_light.c: This property is not compatible with current traffic light configuration.
gui.moretraffic.control_cabinet.title: Control Cabinet Configuration
gui.moretraffic.control_cabinet.instruct:
No instructions present, (for now)
Add them with the plus button!
Executes next action after a set amount of seconds (can be a decimal)
Pauses execution until the block receives a redstone signal
Modifies the light at the position
Modifies the display at the position
gui.moretraffic.control_cabinet.export.i: Choose what to include in export
gui.moretraffic.control_cabinet.repeat.m: Currently, when one iteration ends, it starts over
Currently, when one iteration ends, it stops
gui.moretraffic.control_cabinet.groups.m: Groups allow you to group lights from this mod to different groups and then modify them.
gui.moretraffic.control_cabinet.groups.c:
Create a new group and name it, add in light to target (you can use the /copypos command to copy coordinates easily).
Then you can modify lights in this group using the ‘Modify light’ instruction.
gui.moretraffic.control_cabinet.groups.a: Right Click to add the position in clipboard to group
moretraffic.configuration.title: MoreTraffic Configs
